name: Solar Farm Inverter Compromise
description: Attack a utility-scale solar farm by compromising grid-tied inverters and their SCADA management platform to destabilize grid frequency, cause reverse power flow, or disable generation capacity.
root_objective: Compromise solar farm inverters to destabilize grid frequency or disable generation

nodes.title: Destabilize Grid via Solar Farm Inverter Manipulation
nodes.description: Gain control of grid-tied inverters at a utility-scale solar farm to rapidly disconnect generation, inject reactive power, or oscillate frequency response — causing grid instability or protective relay cascading.

nodes.title: Compromise Inverter Cloud Management Platform
nodes.description: Gain access to the vendor's cloud portal (SolarEdge, SMA Sunny Portal, Huawei FusionSolar) to mass-control inverters remotely.

nodes.title: Direct Site Network Attack on Inverter Controllers
nodes.description: Access the on-site Modbus TCP / SunSpec network to send commands directly to inverters, bypassing cloud management.

nodes.title: Credential Stuffing on Vendor Cloud Portal
nodes.description: Use leaked credentials to access the vendor's inverter management portal. Many installers reuse default or weak passwords across sites.

nodes.title: Exploit Vendor API Vulnerabilities
nodes.description: Target unauthenticated or poorly-authenticated REST APIs exposed by the inverter cloud platform for fleet management operations.

nodes.title: Push Malicious Firmware to Inverter Fleet
nodes.description: Use cloud management access to push crafted firmware updates that alter inverter behaviour — e.g., disabling anti-islanding protection or frequency ride-through settings.

nodes.title: Compromise Site RTU/Gateway via Cellular Modem
nodes.description: Attack the 4G/LTE cellular gateway that provides remote access to the solar farm's on-site SCADA network.

nodes.title: Send Modbus Commands to Trip Inverters Offline
nodes.description: Issue Modbus TCP write commands to simultaneously disconnect all inverters from the grid, causing a sudden generation loss.

nodes.title: Modbus TCP Lacks Authentication
nodes.description: Standard Modbus TCP has no authentication or encryption. Any device on the local OT network can issue write commands to inverter controllers.

nodes.title: Manipulate Reactive Power Setpoints
nodes.description: Alter inverter VAR/power-factor settings via SunSpec registers to inject destabilising reactive power into the distribution grid.

nodes.title: Obtain Inverter Firmware Signing Keys
nodes.description: Attacker must extract or forge firmware signing keys to pass the inverter's update validation, unless the vendor does not implement code signing.

nodes.title: Cellular Gateway Bridges Internet to OT LAN
nodes.description: The 4G cellular router provides NAT traversal from the internet to the flat OT network where all inverters are networked via Modbus TCP.
